author: f1wow

caption:
WTFWAS THATAT🤣🤣🤣🤣😭
.
Pierre Gasly and Esteban Ocon share one of the most complex, deeply personal rifts in modern motorsport, transitioning from childhood “blood brothers” to bitter Formula One rivals. Growing up just 20 minutes apart in Normandy, France, they became inseparable friends at age six; Ocon even famously convinced Gasly to quit football to try out his go-kart. They spent winters training together on freezing, snow-covered tracks, pushing each other to excel. However, as they entered national and international karting championships, their relationship imploded. Gasly cited a major “turning point” during a 2010 World Cup karting race in Portugal where Ocon deliberately cut a corner to crash into him after Gasly began beating him regularly. The fallout intensified through the junior formulas, creating a frosty dynamic that followed them all the way to the F1 paddock.Despite a well-documented history of bad blood and familial tensions, the pair shocked the grid by becoming teammates at the all-French Alpine team for the 2023 and 2024 seasons. While they maintained a strictly professional boundary and openly admitted they would “never be best friends,” their time as teammates was highly volatile, marred by expensive on-track collisions in Australia (2023) and Monaco (2024). The ultimate poetic resolution to their partnership came at the 2024 Brazilian Grand Prix, where they conquered torrential rain to secure an emotional double podium, finishing 2nd and 3rd. Following Ocon’s departure to Haas, Gasly recently reflected that while they still do not actively speak, he hopes they can finally talk things through in 10 or 20 years.
.
Media via bortelowhores/tt
.

caption:
Fernando Alonso is a 44-year-old Spanish racing driver currently competing in his record-breaking 23rd Formula One season for the Aston Martin Aramco-Honda team. Born in Oviedo, Spain, he is widely revered as one of the most complete and relentless racers in motorsport history, famous for securing back-to-back World Drivers’ Championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006. “Alonso won the 2001 Race of Champions Nations’ Cup, and entered the Indianapolis 500 in 2017, 2019 and 2020,” showcasing an exceptional cross-discipline versatility that also includes two victories at the legendary 24 Hours of Le Mans. As F1’s ultimate veteran, Alonso has achieved 32 Grand Prix victories, 106 podiums, and became the first driver in history to surpass 400 Grand Prix weekends. Known for his fiercely sharp and outspoken nature, he continues to heavily influence the paddock and driver market even as he navigates the complex 2026 technical regulations.
.
Via f1.chaos14/tt

caption:
MOVES THAT ALMOST HAPPENED: “I understand that, without my agreement…”

Imagine a world where Oscar Piastri went to Alpine instead of McLaren in 2023.

A legendary potential pairing cut short by tragedy. Ayrton Senna met with Luca di Montezemolo about a move to Ferrari.

Fernando Alonso was offered a 2-year deal at Red Bull. Disagreements about the length killed the deal. 

Nico Hulkenberg lost out on a seat at Mercedes at the end of 2012. That seat went to Lewis Hamilton.

Ron Dennis tried to get Michael Schumacher at McLaren, but it didn’t work out.

Mark Webber said the major reason he didn’t sign with Ferrari is because they would only offer a one-year deal.

author: f1wow

caption:
Arvid Lindblad is an 18-year-old British-Swedish racing driver currently competing in his debut Formula 1 season for the Racing Bulls team. Born in Surrey, England to a Swedish father and a British-Indian mother, Lindblad is a prominent member of the Red Bull Junior Team, having been signed to the prestigious programme at just 14 years old. Recognized as a generational talent, his junior career was defined by record-breaking milestones, including becoming the youngest-ever race winner in both the FIA Formula 3 and FIA Formula 2 championships. Mentored by former Formula E World Champion Oliver Rowland, Lindblad bypassed traditional timelines to earn a full-time seat alongside Liam Lawson for the 2026 F1 season. In doing so, he became the first Formula 1 driver of Indian heritage to compete under the British flag, making a historic debut at the 2026 Australian Grand Prix where he scored points in his very first race.
.
Via Sarah/tt

caption:
🐐

Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver widely regarded as one of the greatest racers in the sport’s history. Born on January 7, 1985, in Stevenage, England, he made his F1 debut in 2007 and quickly established himself with exceptional speed, consistency, and racecraft. Hamilton has won multiple Formula One World Drivers’ Championships, holds numerous records—including the most Grand Prix victories—and has raced for both McLaren and Ferrari after a highly successful period with Mercedes.

Beyond racing, Hamilton is known for his advocacy on diversity, inclusion, environmental sustainability, and animal welfare. He is also involved in fashion, music, and philanthropy, making him one of the most influential figures in global motorsport.

author: f1humor.official

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!

George Russell and Kimi Antonelli walked into anpE interview with two kids, and somehow, the room ended up with four children. G From the moment the first question was asked, it was pure chaos - George, trying to be the mature one, asked a young fan, „How old do you think I am?“ The kid didn’t even blink: „46.“ George’s soul left his body while Kimi absolutely lost it, cackling like a hyena who’d just watched his teammate get publicly executed by a 7-year-old. At that point, the professionalism crumbled.

George was spiraling. Kimi was crying with laughter. The kids were just happy to be there. And honestly? No one was in charge anymore. Four children, two microphones, zero adults, and one of the funniest interviews Formula 1 has ever seen. But what made it even better was the unmistakable brotherly energy between Kimi and George - the kind where you roast each other mercilessly but also have each other’s backs when things get weird.

Kimi wasn’t just laughing at George; he was laughing with him, in that way that only teammates who’ve been through the trenches together can. And George, despite the public humiliation, couldn’t stay mad because deep down, he knew Kimi would’ve done the same if the roles were reversed. They’re not just teammates - they’re the chaotic duo of the paddock, the older brother and younger brother who argue over everything but would defend each other in a heartbeat. And in that room, surrounded by two kids who had no idea who they were, George and Kimi were just two mates having the time of their lives, proving that even in the high-pressure world of F1, the best moments are the ones where you forget you’re a driver and just act like a kid again.
author: 5secondpenalty

author: f1wow

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!

Kimi Antonelli continues to establish himself as
Formula 1’s brightest young star. Arriving in Barcelona chasing a sixth consecutive victory, the Mercedes driver once again showed impressive pace before suffering a heartbreaking late retirement with a mechanical failure after overtaking teammate George Russell for second place.

What makes Antonelli so impressive is his composure under pressure. Even after a difficult Friday where he admitted the weekend would be „not going to be easy,“ the Italian fought his way to the front and looked set for another podium before reliability denied him a strong finish. His consistency has kept him at the center of the championship fight.

Championships aren’t won without setbacks, and Barcelona proved that even the fastest drivers can be stopped by mechanical issues. Antonelli’s talent has never been in doubt, and his response to adversity will be another important chapter in his remarkable season.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!

During the 2013 Malaysian Grand Prix at Sepang, one of Formula’s most infamous team-order controversies unfolded on the podium after Sebastian Vettel defied Red Bull’s „Multi 21“ instruction to hold position behind teammate Mark.

Webber had led comfortably in the closing stages after the final pit stops, conserving his engine and tyres as ordered by the team, while Vettel-starting from pole-closed in aggressively. Despite repeated radio calls from Christian Horner to stay behind („Multi 21, Seb“), Vettel ignored them, launched a risky wheel-to-wheel move, and overtook his teammate to steal the win.

In the cool-down room, a furious Webber confronted Vettel with the icy words „Multi 21, Seb. Multi 21,“ while Vettel looked uncomfortable and later apologised privately. On the podium itself—flanked by a visibly smug third-placed Lewis Hamilton-the atmosphere was painfully awkward: Webber stood with arms folded or stretching in frustration, openly telling interviewer Martin Brundle that Vettel had made his own decisions and would likely receive
„protection as usual“ from the team, while Vettel appeared sheepish, tongue out and hand in pocket, offering a public apology that Webber later dismissed as „easy to do“
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

What looked like the perfect weekend for Kimi Antonelli ended in absolute heartbreak at Silverstone.

After winning the Sprint on Saturday and taking pole position for the British Grand Prix, the Mercedes driver looked like one of the favourites for victory. Although he lost the lead to Charles Leclerc at the start, Antonelli stayed firmly in the fight and was closing the gap during the second stint.

Then disaster struck. After repeatedly attacking the kerbs at Copse, Antonelli suffered damage to the front-left of his Mercedes, leaving the car with a major aerodynamic problem.
His pace disappeared instantly, he dropped through the field, picked up a 5-second penalty for exceeding track limits while struggling with the damaged car, and eventually finished a disappointing 16th.

From Sprint winner and polesitter to leaving Silverstone without a single point... Formula 1 can be unbelievably cruel.

caption:
Tough time never last only tough people last…

————
Charles Leclerc won a chaotic 2026 British Grand Prix for Ferrari, leading a dramatic race that concluded behind the Safety Car following a late-race crash by Max Verstappen. Mercedes’ George Russell secured second place, while Lewis Hamilton overcame an early five-second jump-start penalty to round out the podium in third. Pole-sitter and championship leader Kimi Antonelli suffered severe heartbreak when a mechanical issue with his front-left wheel shield dropped him down the order, ultimately finishing 16th after track limits penalties. Behind the podium finishers, Lando Norris took fourth for McLaren, followed by Red Bull’s Isack Hadjar in fifth, and the Racing Bulls duo of Liam Lawson and Arvid Lindblad in sixth and seventh. Gabriel Bortoleto claimed eighth for Audi, while Alpine secured a double-points finish with Franco Colapinto and Pierre Gasly completing the top ten.

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily f1 content!

At the 2012 Belgian Grand Prix at Spa, the famous Schumacher-Vettel „fake pit“ moment happened on lap 19. Vettel was attacking Michael Schumacher into the Bus Stop chicane and was told by Red Bull to „do the opposite“ of Schumacher’s strategy. As they approached the pit entry, Schumacher appeared to defend the racing line, then suddenly peeled into the pits. Vettel had already committed to the inside and had to take evasive action to avoid contact. After the race, Vettel said it was probably just „a bit of confusion“ rather than a deliberate dangerous move, and the stewards took no further action because Red Bull had admitted they were reacting directly to Schumacher’s pit strategy.

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

In 2018, the Marina Bay circuit was considered a
„weak“ track for Mercedes, with Ferrari and Red Bull expected to dominate. After struggling through practice and barely making it out of Q1, Lewis Hamilton produced a lap in Q3 that defied all team Simulations and logic. Clocking a 1:36.015, Hamilton was over six-tenths faster than his title rival Sebastian Vettel. The lap was a masterclass in precision, as he danced the car inches away from the walls with zero corrections, finding grip where none seemed to exist. Team Principal Toto Wolff famously called it „stardust,“ noting that Hamilton had actually beaten the car’s theoretical limit. It remains one of the most iconic moments in F1 history, effectively breaking the spirit of his rivals and securing a legendary pole position.

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Abu Dhabi. 2010.
Sebastian Vettel crossed the finish line and won the race.
But the championship wasn’t decided yet.
Fernando Alonso and Mark Webber were still on track.
For a few seconds, the entire Red Bull garage waited.
Mechanics watching the timing screens.
Engineers calculating the points.
If Alonso finished high enough, the title was gone.
Then the final positions became clear.
Alonso - seventh.
And the radio finally came alive.
„Thank you boys. Thank you. Unbelievable.“
Then the words that changed everything:
„Sebastian Vettel, you are the world champion! The world champion!“
At 23 years old, Vettel became the youngest Formula 1 world champion in history.
A title decided not just at the finish line -
but in the final seconds of the season.

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

From a smiling underdog to one of the most loved drivers in Formula 1, Daniel Ricciardo made his debut in 2011 and quickly became known for his late-braking „divebomb“ overtakes.

He rose to fame with Red Bull Racing, taking multiple wins and even beating champions on pure skill. His bold move to Renault and later McLaren showed his fearless mindset highlighted by his iconic Monza 2021 win, ending McLaren’s long victory drought Known for his charisma, „shoey“ celebrations and aggressive racing style, Ricciardo isn’t just fast - he’s a fan favorite who brings personality to the grid.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

One of the most famous drives of Lewis Hamilton’s career came at the 2021 So Paulo Grand Prix. After being disqualified from qualifying and taking engine penalties, Hamilton started the sprint race from P20 and stormed through the field to finish P5. He then started the Grand Prix from P10 and produced another remarkable comeback, overtaking rivals one by one before passing Max Verstappen to take victory. Across the sprint and the race, he effectively went from the back of the grid to winning the weekend, a performance widely regarded as one of the greatest comeback drives in modern Formula 1 history.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Formula 1 had always been a proving ground where rookies were expected to learn, adapt, and slowly climb the ladder. But in 2007, Lewis Hamilton tore that script apart. Entering the sport with McLaren, there was no gentle introduction, no easing into the chaos. From the very first race, Hamilton was thrown into the fire - battling world champions, seasoned veterans, and the relentless spotlight of the biggest stage in motorsport.

What followed was nothing short of astonishing.
Podium after podium, race after race, Hamilton displayed a consistency that defied logic for a newcomer. He raced without fear, without hesitation, armed only with raw pace and unshakable belief. The paddock quickly realized this wasn’t a typical rookie story - this was something extraordinary. The shock deepened when Hamilton wasn’t just competing; he was fighting for the championship itself.

Against his own teammate, reigning champion Fernando Alonso, Hamilton engaged in one of the fiercest intra-team duels Formula 1 had ever witnessed. No hierarchy, no deference, no backing down - just pure, uncompromising competition.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Niki Lauda - 3x Formula 1 World Champion (1975, 1977, 1984).

Started his F1 career in 1971 and became one of the most fearless drivers in motorsport history. He won championships with both Scuderia Ferrari and McLaren- something very few drivers ever achieved.

In 1976, Lauda suffered a horrific crash at the Nürburgring. His Ferrari caught fire, leaving him with severe burns and lung damage. Doctors thought he wouldn’t survive... but just 42 days later, he returned to race at Monza and finished P4. One of the greatest comebacks in sports history.

After retiring once, he came back again with McLaren and won his third world title in 1984 by just half a point against Alain Prost.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Carlos Sainz and Charles Leclerc had one of the healthiest teammate relationships in modern Formula 1. They raced together at Ferrari from 2021 to 2024, where they were incredibly closely matched. Both wanted to beat each other every weekend, but neither let that competition damage the respect they had for one another.

Off the track, they became genuine friends. Fans loved seeing them together because their chemistry felt natural... they were constantly joking in Ferrari videos, teasing each other, and sharing plenty of laughs. One of the best examples came after the 2024 Azerbaijan Grand Prix, when travel disruptions meant they rented a van and drove back to Monaco together, with Charles joking that Carlos was a
„10/10 chauffeur.“

Even after Carlos left Ferrari, they continued to speak highly of each other. Charles has said Carlos made him a better driver, while Carlos has described Charles as one of the toughest teammates he has ever had. Their relationship is a great example of two elite competitors pushing each other to the limit while still maintaining a genuine friendship and mutual respect.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Follow 5secondpenalty for daily F1 content!🏎️

Nobody owned Monaco the way Senna did.
Not even close. His relationship with the Monte Carlo street circuit goes beyond six victories, eight podiums, and five pole positions in just ten Grand Prix starts.

It started in 1984. His fifth ever F1 race. In a Toleman
- one of the slowest cars on the grid. Pouring rain.
He was closing on Prost at over two seconds a lap when the race was controversially red flagged. He was robbed. But Monaco had seen enough to know.

1988. The greatest qualifying lap in F1 history
- and not a single TV camera caught it. He out-qualified his McLaren teammate by 1.427 seconds. Then on race day - 50 seconds ahead of Prost and cruising. Then a moment of distraction. He hit the wall at Portier. Walked back to his apartment two blocks away. Sat alone in silence.
That crash haunted him. And fuelled everything that came after. There was something supernatural about Ayrton in Monaco. He described his 1988 qualifying lap himself: „Suddenly I realised I was no longer driving the car consciously. I was in a different dimension.“

1992. Williams had the fastest car by miles.
Mansell built a 30 second lead. Pitted for tyres. Came out behind Senna with fresher rubber and a much quicker car. Senna put on a defensive masterclass — beating Mansell by just 0.215 seconds after holding him off for eight laps.

1993. Starting third behind Prost and Schumacher. Crashed in practice and injured his hand. Still won - for the sixth time. Breaking Graham Hill’s record. Damon Hill finished second and said his father would have been the first to congratulate Senna.

Six wins. Five poles. Eight podiums. Ten starts.
He didn’t race Monaco. He communed with it.
author: 5secondpenalty

caption:
Guess who’s back

————-
At the 2026 Austrian Grand Prix, Scuderia Ferrari HP brought a highly anticipated, upgraded power unit capitalized through the FIA’s performance catch-up mechanism (ADUO). Following Lewis Hamilton’s breakthrough victory for the team in Barcelona two weeks prior, the Italian squad showed strong pace at the short, high-altitude Red Bull Ring. Drivers Charles Leclerc and Hamilton initially locked down impressive second and third place spots on the starting grid during Saturday’s qualifying. However, Sunday’s 71-lap race proved difficult for the Scuderia. Hamilton’s race execution was hampered by an unsuccessful three-stop strategic plan, resulting in a fifth-place finish, while Leclerc crossed the finish line a lap down in eighth place.

caption:
Races where Lewis Hamilton has avoided the Ferrari simulator in 2026:

🇨🇳 China: P3
🇨🇦 Canada: P2
🇲🇨 Monaco: P2
🇪🇸 Spain: P1 🏆

————-
Following his decision to bypass Scuderia Ferrari’s race-preparation simulator due to data correlation issues, Lewis Hamilton experienced a sensational resurgence across the 2026 season, delivering masterful performances in China, Canada, Monaco, and Spain. The streak began at the Chinese Grand Prix, where he skipped the simulator to secure an impressive P3 finish, marking his first-ever podium in Ferrari red. Although he briefly returned to the virtual rig for a frustrating weekend in Miami, Hamilton completely abandoned it afterward, immediately reaping the rewards at the Canadian Grand Prix with a brilliant P2 podium finish. He carried this old-school momentum directly to the streets of Monte Carlo for the Monaco Grand Prix, skipping the simulator once more to secure another stellar P2 finish and equal Ayrton Senna’s historic record of eight career podiums in the Principality. This incredible run culminated at the Spanish Grand Prix in Barcelona, where a perfectly dialed-in car allowed Hamilton to capture a commanding P1 victory, sealing his emotional maiden race win for Scuderia Ferrari.

#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #scuderiaferrari #charlesleclerc

caption:
It’s getting tight at the top 

———-
The Formula 1 Drivers’ Championship has suddenly tightened into a thrilling battle between championship leader Kimi Antonelli and seven-time world champion Lewis Hamilton. Following a dramatic race weekend at the 2026 Barcelona Grand Prix, Antonelli’s massive 66-point lead was slashed down to just 41 points. Hamilton capitalized on a late mechanical DNF from the young Italian rookie to storm to his magnificent maiden victory for Ferrari, collecting a maximum 25 points. With Antonelli still holding the top spot at 156 points and Hamilton surging in second with 115 points, this unexpected swing has injected fresh momentum into the title race, proving that the battle for the 2026 crown is far from over.
#formula1 #f1memes #lewishamilton #kimiantonelli #racing

caption:
Our season starts this week! 
Join us on Patreon on Tuesday, 3rd March, for a close look at all the details in our 2026 liveries. 

And on Youtube on Wednesday, 4th March for our season opener episode. (It’s the longest video we’ve ever made(/making, still working on it).

This marks the first major evolutionary upgrade for our characters since 2020. Excited for a new season! 

#f1 #formula1 #australiangp
